title: NOC 21332 – Petroleum Engineers | GTR Immigration
description: Petroleum Engineers are in strong demand across Canada, driven by ongoing energy projects, offshore development, enhanced-recovery programs, and advanced drilling technologies. Classified under NOC 21332 (TEER 1), this occupation is well suited for:
duties: Design drilling programs and select fluids & bits
Supervise well completion, testing, and surveys
Analyze reservoir rock and fluid data
Oversee installation and maintenance of equipment
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in petroleum engineering or related field
Master’s/PhD for research or senior roles (sometimes)
job_outlook: This indicates favourable prospects for internationally trained Petroleum Engineers—especially those with:
pathways: Career mobility into hydrogen & carbon-capture sectors
additional_information: Energy operators and engineering consultancies recruit Petroleum Engineers for:
Drilling & well-completion programsOffshore and subsea projectsReservoir modeling & forecastingEnhanced oil recovery (EOR)Corrosion control systemsDigital-oilfield & automation projects
Annual salaries often range from $58,900–$151,900 CAD, depending on province, offshore exposure, and licensing status.
